title: Zanzibar 5-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Zanzibar 5-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Zanzibar, Tanzania: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Stone Town guided walking tour and Prison Island tour. Prices from $1729 per person (estimate).
highlights: Overwater-style suite at a private beach resort
Private beach dinner on the sand
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Zanzibar, Tanzania — Tropical climate — warm and humid year-round (highs 28-32°C), with a long rainy season (March-May) and a shorter one (November). June to October for the dry season with lower humidity; December to February also offers good weather between rainy periods. This itinerary blends Stone Town guided walking tour, Prison Island tour, Stone Town's carved doors with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Stone Town guided walking tour, Prison Island tour, Stone Town's carved doors
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Zanzibar
itinerary.description: Land at Abeid Amani Karume International Airport (ZNZ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Stone Town guided walking tour — UNESCO-listed old town of carved wooden doors and winding alleys. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Zanzibar: Prison Island tour & Spice farm tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Prison Island tour — Boat trip to see giant Aldabra tortoises and snorkel. In the afternoon, continue to Spice farm tour — Guided walk through a working clove, vanilla, and spice plantation. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Zanzibar: Jozani Forest & Safari Blue sailing and snorkeling day trip
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Jozani Forest — Home to the endemic red colobus monkey. In the afternoon, continue to Safari Blue sailing and snorkeling day trip — Dhow sailing trip with snorkeling and a seafood lunch on a sandbank. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Safari Blue sailing and snorkeling day trip

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Prison Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Prison Island, a popular side trip from Zanzibar. Return to Zanzibar in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Stone Town's carved doors or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to ZNZ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Combine 2-3 days in Stone Town with several beach days on the north or east coast for the best of both.
Book Mnemba Atoll snorkeling or diving through a reputable operator — it's a protected, high-quality reef.
Bring cash — many smaller restaurants and shops outside resorts don't take cards.
Visit the spice farms in the morning when the plantation is most active.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing for Stone Town and inland villages
Light, breathable fabrics for the heat and humidity
thingsToDo: Safari Blue sailing and snorkeling day trip
thingsToAvoid: Wearing swimwear or revealing clothing in Stone Town or local villages.
Skipping reef-safe sunscreen — regular sunscreen damages the coral reefs.
Underestimating travel time between Stone Town and the beach resort areas.
topRestaurants: The Rock Restaurant — Seafood, on a rock islet above the sea ($$$$)
Forodhani Gardens food stalls — Zanzibari street food ($)
Emerson Spice Tea House — Rooftop Swahili fine dining ($$$)
instagramSpots: The Rock Restaurant at high tide
luxuryExperiences: Overwater-style suite at a private beach resort
honeymoonExperiences: Private beach dinner on the sand
Sunset dhow sail along the coast
Couples spa treatment at a beachfront resort
localEtiquette: Zanzibar is predominantly Muslim — dress modestly away from resort beaches, especially in Stone Town.
Ask permission before photographing local people.
Alcohol is available at resorts and tourist restaurants but not universally, given the island's religious character.
localTransportGuide: Stone Town is entirely walkable, but reaching the north or east coast beach areas (Nungwi, Kendwa, Paje) requires a taxi, resort shuttle, or a shared minibus (dalla-dalla) for budget travelers. Roads are basic in places, so journeys can take longer than the distance suggests.
bestTimeToVisit: June to October for the dry season with lower humidity; December to February also offers good weather between rainy periods.
weather: Tropical climate — warm and humid year-round (highs 28-32°C), with a long rainy season (March-May) and a shorter one (November).
visaInformation: Tanzania offers an e-visa or visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India — arranging the e-visa in advance is recommended to avoid arrival queues.
averageFlightTime: ~11.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Zanzibar?
faqs.answer: Tanzania offers an e-visa or visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India — arranging the e-visa in advance is recommended to avoid arrival queues.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Zanzibar?
faqs.answer: June to October for the dry season with lower humidity; December to February also offers good weather between rainy periods. Tropical climate — warm and humid year-round (highs 28-32°C), with a long rainy season (March-May) and a shorter one (November).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Zanzibar safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Zanzibar is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Zanzibar?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TZS. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Zanzibar is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private beach dinner on the sand experience.

reviews.author: Marco Williams
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Jozani Forest and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Isabella Rossi
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Forodhani Gardens night food market and Prison Island tour. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Wang
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Safari Blue sailing and snorkeling day trip alone was worth the trip, but combined with Forodhani Gardens night food market this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sarah Tanaka
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Zanzibar is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Stone Town's carved doors. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: James Khan
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to Zanzibar was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Stone Town's carved doors was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
